title_en: Marriage and Commercial Law in Al-Mabsut
content_en:
As-Sarakhsi's chapters on marriage and commercial transactions in Al-Mabsut represent the fullest classical exposition of Hanafi private law. His comparative engagement with other schools is at its most sophisticated in these chapters, as the schools differ most significantly in their treatment of personal and commercial legal matters.

On marriage, as-Sarakhsi presents the Hanafi position that an adult woman of sound mind may contract her own marriage as a validation of her full legal capacity in all matters. He engages at length with the Shafi'i requirement of a wali, presenting their evidential arguments (particularly the hadith 'There is no marriage without a guardian') and then arguing that the Hanafi interpretation — that the hadith refers to recommendation and social propriety, not a validity condition — is supported by the Quranic verse directly addressing women as autonomous contracting parties: 'When you have divorced your wives and they have completed their waiting periods, do not prevent them from marrying their husbands' (2:232).

The Hanafi school's minimum mahr of ten dirhams is defended with reference to the reported practices of the Companions and the principle that a mahr must have material significance to fulfill its social function. As-Sarakhsi notes that the spiritual significance of the mahr — as a gift of honor from the groom to the bride, distinguishing Islamic marriage from mere cohabitation — requires that it be of some genuine value.

In commercial law, as-Sarakhsi's analysis of the bay' al-fasid (irregular but not void sale) is one of the Hanafi school's most distinctive contributions. Unlike the Shafi'i school's binary of valid and void transactions, the Hanafi school has a tripartite system: sahih (valid), fasid (irregular — contractually complete but legally incomplete), and batil (void). A fasid transaction is binding in some respects (title passes upon possession) but remains legally defective until the defect is cured. As-Sarakhsi develops this three-tier system with numerous examples and defends it against the Shafi'i critique.

The law of salam (forward sale) receives extensive treatment. As-Sarakhsi analyzes each of the conditions for valid salam: specification of the commodity, specification of the delivery date, specification of the delivery place, payment in full at the time of contracting, and the commodity being something that exists in standard, measurable form. He notes the important Hanafi position that specifying the delivery place is a necessary condition for salam — a stricter requirement than some other schools.

As-Sarakhsi concludes the commercial law chapters by invoking the Quranic framework: 'Allah has permitted trade and forbidden riba' (2:275). This simple verse, he argues, captures the fundamental principle of Islamic commercial law — commerce based on genuine exchange and mutual benefit is the divinely approved path to prosperity, while riba (guaranteed return without productive activity or risk) is the paradigmatic violation of this principle. The scholar's task is to distinguish, with precision and wisdom, between the vast range of trade that Allah has permitted and the forms of exchange that His wisdom has prohibited.
title_id: Hukum Perkawinan dan Muamalat dalam Al-Mabsut
content_id:
Bab-bab as-Sarakhsi tentang perkawinan dan transaksi komersial dalam Al-Mabsut merupakan eksposisi klasik paling lengkap tentang hukum privat Hanafi. Keterlibatan komparatifnya dengan mazhab-mazhab lain paling canggih dalam bab-bab ini, karena perbedaan antara mazhab Hanafi dan Syafi'i sangat signifikan dalam bidang-bidang ini.

Dalam hukum perkawinan, as-Sarakhsi membahas syarat-syarat sah nikah, peran wali, mahar, hak-hak dan kewajiban suami istri, serta berbagai bentuk talak dan khul'. Posisi khas Hanafi bahwa seorang wanita dewasa dapat menikahkan dirinya sendiri tanpa memerlukan wali (bertentangan dengan mazhab Syafi'i) mendapat pembelaan panjang dengan mengacu pada dalil-dalil yang memperkuatnya.

Pembahasan tentang talak sangat terperinci, mencakup talak raj'i dan ba'in, talak tiga dalam satu ucapan, talak yang diumurkan, 'iddah, dan nafkah selama 'iddah. As-Sarakhsi menganalisis berbagai formula talak yang digunakan oleh orang awam dan konsekuensi hukumnya masing-masing, sebuah pembahasan yang sangat praktis dan relevan untuk pengadilan agama.

Dalam transaksi komersial, as-Sarakhsi mencakup jual beli, sewa-menyewa (ijarah), musyarakah, mudharabah, dan berbagai kontrak lainnya. Beliau sangat teliti dalam membahas syarat-syarat keabsahan kontrak, kondisi-kondisi yang membuatnya fasid (rusak), dan hak-hak para pihak ketika terjadi perselisihan. Pendekatan Hanafi yang pragmatis terhadap kontrak komersial — yang cenderung lebih fleksibel dari Syafi'i dalam beberapa hal — mendapat pembelaan yang kuat dan metodis dari as-Sarakhsi, menjadikan Al-Mabsut sebagai panduan yang tak ternilai untuk fiqh muamalat Hanafi.
content_ar:
أبواب النكاح والمعاملات في المبسوط تمثّل النصف الأوسع من الكتاب، وفيها يظهر براعة السرخسي الفقهية في أكمل صورها. ففي النكاح يُفصّل شروط صحته وأركانه وما يُعدّ ولياً وما لا يُعدّ، مُبيّناً موقف المذهب الحنفي من ولاية المرأة على نفسها، وهي من أبرز مسائل الخلاف بين الحنفية والشافعية، إذ يجيز الحنفية للمرأة البالغة العاقلة أن تُزوّج نفسها بغير ولي.

وأحكام المهر والنفقة والخلع والطلاق وأنواعه تحتل مساحة واسعة. ومسألة طلاق الثلاث بلفظ واحد وما إذا كان يقع ثلاثاً أو واحدة تُعدّ من أكثر المسائل التي يُوضّح فيها السرخسي موقف أبي حنيفة ودليله. كما يتناول أحكام الإيلاء والظهار واللعان بتوسع كبير.

وفي المعاملات يبسط السرخسي أحكام البيع وشروط صحته وخياراته وما يُفسده من الغرر والجهالة. وأبواب الربا من أكثر الأبواب التي يُظهر فيها منهجه المقارن، إذ يستعرض أنواع الربا وأدلة كل قول، مُبيّناً الأصناف الستة الربوية ومسوّغات التفاضل والتماثل. وتُكمّل أبواب الإجارة والشركة والمضاربة والرهن والكفالة والحوالة منظومة المعاملات في هذا الكتاب النفيس.
